segments.title: Kawano turned tenure into procurement influence
segments.summary: The opening frames Admiral Katsutoshi Kawano as a long-serving Joint Staff chief whose political trust helped embed Japanese naval procurement decisions.
segments.body: Kawano's unusually long tenure, relationship with Prime Minister Abe, and diplomatic skill let him move procurement ideas into the political structure without requiring a public victory lap.

evidence.note: The transcript identifies Kawano as the long-serving exception among Joint Staff chiefs and links his tenure to political trust and decision continuity.
The transcript says current Japanese naval procurement owes much to Kawano's political work embedding long-term decisions.
The transcript explains that Japanese command culture lets successors put prepared ideas through as part of the national-interest process.
